title: Bulk enqueue with per-job unique constraints (basic engine limitation)
prompt: A naive implementation writes `Oban.insert_all(Enum.map(ids, &Worker.new(%{id: &1}, unique: [period: 60, keys: [:id]])))` and expects Oban to dedupe. With the basic engine (free Oban), `insert_all/1` does NOT enforce unique constraints — only the Smart Engine in Oban Pro does. Rewrite `MyApp.Enqueuer.enqueue_batch/1` (takes a list of `ids`) so it uses a per-id `Oban.insert/1` loop instead of `insert_all/1`. Each `MyApp.Workers.ProcessWorker.new(%{"id" => id}, unique: [period: 60, keys: [:id]])` should be inserted individually. Return a list of `{:ok, job} | {:error, reason}` tuples, one per id. Include a comment citing the basic-engine limitation. Write to `lib/my_app/enqueuer.ex`.
tier_rationale: Knowing that insert_all has NO unique enforcement on the basic engine is a direct docs citation but almost always missed. Sonnet defaults to insert_all for bulk operations.
research_citation: Oban module HexDocs — 'Only the Smart Engine in Oban Pro supports bulk unique jobs… With the basic engine, you must use insert/3 to insert unique jobs one at a time.'
